name: HMS Lord Essenden (FY 218)
events: 1 Dec 1941 HMS P 36 (Lt. H.N. Edmonds, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Dartmouth with HMS Cleveland (Cdr. G.B. Sayer, RN), HMS Coriolanus (T/S.Lt. I.R. Saddler, RNVR), HMS Pearl (T/Lt. G.K. Hill, RNVR) and HMS Lord Essenden (Skr. J.T. Denoon, RNR).
HMS P 36 also carried out gunnery exercises on a target that was being towed by HMS Mangrove (T/Lt. J.K.M. Warde, RNVR) (1)

26 Jun 1942 Around 1015 B/26, HMS Torbay (Cdr. A.C.C. Miers, DSO and Bar, RN) departed from Portsmouth bound for the Devonport Dockyard at Plymouth where she is to refit. She arrived at Plymouth around 0840 B/26. She was escorted by HMS Lord Essenden (Skr. J.W. Shell, RNR). Most of the passage was made in a coastal convoy. (2)

6 Mar 1943 HMS P 511 (Lt. C.R. Pelly, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with HMS Rowan (T/Lt. R.O. Moore, RNVR), HMS Lord Essenden (A/Skr.Lt. F.A. Yeomans, RNR), HMS Cape Comorin (T/Lt. N.L. Brown, RNVR) and HMS Ambrose Pare (Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR). (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
